_README: CVE DATA - EDIT THIS FILE, NOT THE HTA. The tool ships no CVE facts in its code.
THIS FILE IS THE FALLBACK, NOT THE PRIMARY SOURCE. With the live check on (default), the tool asks
NVD which CVEs affect the detected product+version and takes the fix builds from Broadcom's own CNA
data, which NVD republishes free and keyless. Vendor data beats anything typed in here, so it wins.
What NVD does NOT cover, and this file therefore still answers:
- older advisories filed before structured version data was the norm (no version rows at all)
- CVEs carrying a fixed build for one release line but not the other
- vCenter entries that give an update LABEL only (e.g. '8.0 U3e') with no build to compare
- the two config cross-checks NVD cannot do: SLP running state, AD join
- any host with no network: untick the live check and this file is all there is
If the live check finds a fix build that DISAGREES with one below, the vendor is used and the row
says so loudly. That means the entry here is wrong - fix it.
HOW TO UPDATE:
1. Open the advisory index: https://www.broadcom.com/support/vmware-security-advisories
2. For each CVE below, confirm the fixed build for the affected line against its advisory.
3. Correct the numbers, add new entries, and set "reviewed" to today (YYYY-MM-DD).
4. Set "reviewedBy" to your initials so the report can say who last checked.
The tool warns in the UI and in the report once "reviewed" is older than staleAfterDays.
PROVENANCE WARNING: these values were originally authored from an AI model's memory. The live check
has since corroborated the 2025-era builds against the vendor's own data, but the older entries have
NOT been confirmed by a human and NVD carries nothing to check them against. Treat those as
unverified until you have read the advisory and stamped "reviewed"/"reviewedBy" yourself.
rule = how the verdict is reached:
slp   - compare build vs fix, and cross-check the SLP service state (mitigated if not running)
ad    - no build compare; verdict depends on whether the host is AD-integrated
line  - ESXi: pick the fix build from THIS host's version + update level (builds are NOT
monotonic across trains, e.g. 8.0 U3f 24784735 < 8.0 U2e 24789317)
multi - vCenter: only a build number is exposed and 7.0/8.0 ranges overlap, so compare
conservatively vs every fix build (patched only if >= the highest)

esxi.title: OpenSLP heap overflow RCE (ESXiArgs mass ransomware)
ESXi Active Directory 'ESX Admins' auth bypass (abused by ransomware)
VMCI TOCTOU out-of-bounds write - guest-to-host VMX escape (EXPLOITED IN THE WILD 2025-03)

line.v9: not affected (ESX 9.x shipped after the fix)
fixed in ESX 9.0.0.0100 - verify this 9.x build vs the VMSA

vcsa.title: vCenter DCERPC out-of-bounds write - unauth RCE (exploited in the wild)
vCenter DCERPC heap overflow - unauth RCE
vCenter authenticated command execution via alarm script actions
